function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'foo'?

loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'j < 2' is never executed?

dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 6:  <statement> int i, j; </statement> is reached, the statement line 11:  <statement> count += 2; </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 14:  <statement> int </statement> dominate line 18:  <statement> printf ("The value of count is %d\n", count); </statement>?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable count at line 11: <statement>       count += 2;  </statement> depend on the value of variable count at line 10: <statement>         count += 1;  </statement>?
Does variable j at line 7: <statement>   for (j = 0; j < 2; j++) </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 9: <statement>       for (i = 0; i < 2; i++)  </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'i' live at the end of function 'foo'?
Is variable 'j' live at the end of function 'foo'?
dead_code: Is this statement not dead: “printf ("The value of count is %d\n", count);”?
